rationale: This page provides a structured overview of Indian recipients of the Ramon Magsaysay Award, categorized by field (Public Service, Journalism, Social Work). It also highlights a significant recent update regarding the first Indian NGO to receive the award, which is highly relevant for current affairs and history prelims.
cleanedText:
Ramon Magsaysay Award
Indian Recipients (Individuals & Organisation)
General Information:
- Location: Philippines
- Awarding Body: RMAF (Ramon Magsaysay Award Foundation)
- Known as: "Asia's Nobel Prize"
Public Service / Governance Recipients:
- Vinoba Bhave (1958) - Bhoodan Movement
- Verghese Kurien (1963) - White Revolution
- M.S. Subbulakshmi (1974) - Music & Charity
- Others: Anna Hazare, JRD Tata, Aruna Roy, N.R. Murthy...
Journalism Recipients:
- R.K. Laxman (1984) - Cartoons
- P. Sainath (2007) - Rural Reporting
- Ravish Kumar (2019) - Media Ethics
Social Work Recipients:
- Ela Bhatt (1977) - SEWA
- Bindeshwar Pathak (2009) - Sulabh
- Others: Sandeep Pandey, Harsh Mander...
LATEST: First Indian NGO - Educate Girls
- Award Year: 2025
- Founder: Safeena Husain
- Mission: Educate Rural Girls
- Key Pillars: Enrol, Empower, Educate
- Focus: Learning Outcomes, D.I.B. Model
Prelims Facts:
- Latest Individual Recipient: Ravish Kumar (2019)
- Latest NGO Recipient: Educate Girls (2025)
- Note: Educate Girls is the ONLY Indian Organization to win the Ramon Magsaysay Award.
